{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"
<context_window_protection>
  <priority_instructions>
    Raw tool output floods context window. MUST use context-mode MCP tools. Keep raw data in sandbox.
  </priority_instructions>

  <tool_selection_hierarchy>
    1. GATHER: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_batch_execute(commands, queries)
       - Primary research tool. Runs commands, auto-indexes, searches. ONE call replaces many steps.
       - Each command: {label: "section header", command: "shell command"}
       - label becomes FTS5 chunk title — descriptive labels improve search.
    2. FOLLOW-UP: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_search(queries: ["q1", "q2", ...])
       - All follow-up questions. ONE call, many queries.
    3. PROCESSING: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute(language, code) | mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file(path, language, code)
       - API calls, log analysis, data processing.
  </tool_selection_hierarchy>

  <forbidden_actions>
    - NO Bash for commands producing >20 lines output.
    - NO Read for analysis — use execute_file. Read IS correct for files you intend to Edit.
    - NO WebFetch — use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_fetch_and_index.
    - Bash ONLY for git/mkdir/rm/mv/navigation.
    - NO mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute or mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file for file creation/modification.
      ctx_execute is for analysis, processing, computation only.
  </forbidden_actions>

  <file_writing_policy>
    ALWAYS use native Write/Edit tools for file creation/modification.
    NEVER use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute, mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file, or Bash to write files.
    Applies to all file types: code, configs, plans, specs, YAML, JSON, markdown.
  </file_writing_policy>

  <output_constraints>
    <communication_style>
      Terse like caveman. Technical substance exact. Only fluff die.
      Use fragments when clear. Short synonyms (fix not "implement a solution for").
      Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged.
      Auto-expand for: security warnings, irreversible actions, user confusion.
    </communication_style>
    <artifact_policy>
      Write artifacts (code, configs, PRDs) to FILES. NEVER inline.
      Return only: file path + 1-line description.
    </artifact_policy>
    <response_format>
      Concise summary:
      - Actions taken (2-3 bullets)
      - File paths created/modified
      - Key findings
    </response_format>
  </output_constraints>

  <ctx_commands>
    "ctx stats" | "ctx-stats" | "/ctx-stats" | context savings question
    → Call stats MCP tool, display full output verbatim.

    "ctx doctor" | "ctx-doctor" | "/ctx-doctor" | diagnose context-mode
    → Call doctor MCP tool, run returned shell command, display as checklist.

    "ctx upgrade" | "ctx-upgrade" | "/ctx-upgrade" | update context-mode
    → Call upgrade MCP tool, run returned shell command, display as checklist.

    "ctx purge" | "ctx-purge" | "/ctx-purge" | wipe/reset knowledge base
    → Call purge MCP tool with confirm: true. Warn: irreversible.

    After /clear or /compact: knowledge base preserved. Tell user: "context-mode knowledge base preserved. Use `ctx purge` to start fresh."
  </ctx_commands>

</context_window_protection>"}}

<context_window_protection>

    1. GATHER: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_batch_execute(commands, queries)

       - label becomes FTS5 chunk title — descriptive labels improve search.
    2. FOLLOW-UP: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_search(queries: ["q1", "q2", ...])
       - All follow-up questions. ONE call, many queries.
    3. PROCESSING: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute(language, code) | mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file(path, language, code)
       - API calls, log analysis, data processing.

  <forbidden_actions>
    - NO Bash for commands producing >20 lines output.
    - NO Read for analysis — use execute_file. Read IS correct for files you intend to Edit.
    - NO WebFetch — use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_fetch_and_index.
    - Bash ONLY for git/mkdir/rm/mv/navigation.
    - NO mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute or mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file for file creation/modification.
      ctx_execute is for analysis, processing, computation only.
  </forbidden_actions>

  <file_writing_policy>
    ALWAYS use native Write/Edit tools for file creation/modification.
    NEVER use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute, mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file, or Bash to write files.
    Applies to all file types: code, configs, plans, specs, YAML, JSON, markdown.
  </file_writing_policy>

  <output_constraints>
    <communication_style>
      Terse like caveman. Technical substance exact. Only fluff die.
      Use fragments when clear. Short synonyms (fix not "implement a solution for").
      Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged.
      Auto-expand for: security warnings, irreversible actions, user confusion.
    </communication_style>
    <artifact_policy>
      Write artifacts (code, configs, PRDs) to FILES. NEVER inline.
      Return only: file path + 1-line description.
    </artifact_policy>
    <response_format>
      Concise summary:
      - Actions taken (2-3 bullets)
      - File paths created/modified
      - Key findings
    </response_format>
  </output_constraints>

  <ctx_commands>
    "ctx stats" | "ctx-stats" | "/ctx-stats" | context savings question
    → Call stats MCP tool, display full output verbatim.

    "ctx doctor" | "ctx-doctor" | "/ctx-doctor" | diagnose context-mode
    → Call doctor MCP tool, run returned shell command, display as checklist.

    "ctx upgrade" | "ctx-upgrade" | "/ctx-upgrade" | update context-mode
    → Call upgrade MCP tool, run returned shell command, display as checklist.

    → Call purge MCP tool with confirm: true. Warn: irreversible.

    After /clear or /compact: knowledge base preserved. Tell user: "context-mode knowledge base preserved. Use `ctx purge` to start fresh."

</context_window_protection>"}}

content.thinking: The user wants me to write a 100-150 word opening hook for a technical blog post and save it to hook.md. Let me write this directly without asking questions.

input.content: Your engineers aren't waiting on code reviews. They're waiting on CI. That 22-minute pipeline nobody questions anymore? It's not a technical problem — it's a $400,000-a-year tax on your engineering organization. At 100 engineers, even a 15-minute average wait, twice a day, compounds into thousands of hours of broken flow state annually. The cruel irony is that the teams suffering most are the ones moving fastest: more commits, more PRs, more collisions on a pipeline that was designed for a team a third the size. Slow CI isn't a symptom of growth — it becomes the ceiling on it. The fixes are rarely exotic. They're a handful of structural decisions made years ago that nobody has had the mandate to revisit. Until now.

content.thinking:
The hook is written and saved to hook.md. Let me count the words to make sure it's in the 100-150 range.

"Your engineers aren't waiting on code reviews. They're waiting on CI. That 22-minute pipeline nobody questions anymore? It's not a technical problem — it's a $400,000-a-year tax on your engineering organization. At 100 engineers, even a 15-minute average wait, twice a day, compounds into thousands of hours of broken flow state annually. The cruel irony is that the teams suffering most are the ones moving fastest: more commits, more PRs, more collisions on a pipeline that was designed for a team a third the size. 

Slow CI becomes the growth ceiling itself — it's not a symptom, it's the constraint. The solutions aren't complicated; they're structural decisions made years ago that nobody had the mandate to challenge until now.

Your engineers aren't blocked on code reviews. They're blocked on CI. That 22-minute pipeline that's become invisible? It's a $400,000 annual tax on a 100-person engineering org. Even a modest 15-minute wait twice daily bleeds thousands of hours of lost flow state every year. The worst part is that the fastest-moving teams suffer most — more commits, more PRs, more pipeline collisions on infrastructure designed for a third the current headcount.

Slow CI stops being a symptom of growth and becomes the actual constraint on it. The solutions aren't complicated. They're usually a few architectural choices made years ago that nobody's had the authority to revisit. Until now.

content.text: Saved to `hook.md`. The hook runs ~121 words — opens with a reframe (the wait isn't reviews, it's CI), quantifies the cost in terms EMs care about (dollars and flow state), then escalates to the structural argument before landing on the implicit promise that there's a fix.
